tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Almost all fail-to-pass tests use grep to check for text patterns in source files (e.g., grep for 'b.ts - a.ts', grep for 'toBeNull', grep for 'export.*findMostRecentTaskForWorkspace'). Only test_task_history_ts_compiles_with_tsc and test_repo_vitest_utils_tests actually execute code. The core tests are text-matching, not behavior verification.

test_not_tautological.reason: Many tests are grep-based and could be satisfied by creating files with the right text patterns without implementing actual functionality. For example, test_find_most_recent_task_for_workspace_sorts_by_timestamp just greps for 'b.ts - a.ts' — a comment containing that string would pass. test_task_history_test_has_null_for_no_match just greps for 'toBeNull' — any occurrence would pass. Only test_repo_vitest_utils_tests actually runs the tests.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution/solve.sh is visible in the container at /workspace and reveals the exact gold commit SHA (bce71b4448815975d9192d60432b694ddcd5fa03). An agent could simply read solve.sh and run 'git checkout bce71b4448815975d9192d60432b694ddcd5fa03' to pass all tests. Additionally, since tests are mostly grep-based, the agent could grep the git history for the expected patterns.
